title: Basic
summary: Defines the formal Poincaré–Einstein space, its bulk and boundary dimensions, compactification data, and Einstein condition.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines the formal Poincaré–Einstein space, its bulk and boundary dimensions, compactification data, and Einstein condition.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/PE/Basic.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: PESpace. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Formal proposition: bulk Dim eq boundary Dim add one. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: Function Symbol. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: Metric Symbol. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Definition: scale By Function. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByFunction (a : FunctionSymbol X) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: scale By Scalar. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def scaleByScalar (a : Real) (g : MetricSymbol X) : MetricSymbol X

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Conformal Infinity. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
Data/interface: PEMetric Data. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.

correspondence.leanType: def einsteinRHS (X : PESpace) (gPlus : MetricSymbol X.bulk) : MetricSymbol X.bulk

references.note: Source for Poincaré normal form and its relation to straight ambient metrics.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
